title: Pega Customer Service — Complete Deep Dive
content:
# Pega Customer Service — Complete Deep Dive

## What is Pega Customer Service?
Pega Customer Service (PCS) is Pega's industry-leading customer service application that enables organizations to deliver consistent, efficient, and personalized service across all channels. Built on the Pega Platform, PCS combines case management, AI-powered decisioning, and process automation to help agents resolve customer issues faster while providing self-service options for customers.

### Key Value Propositions
- **Omnichannel**: Unified agent desktop handling phone, chat, email, messaging, social media, and web
- **AI-powered**: Next-Best-Action guidance, email auto-generation, sentiment analysis, knowledge suggestions
- **Case-centric**: Every interaction is managed as a case with full lifecycle tracking
- **Process automation**: Guided workflows reduce agent training time and ensure consistent service delivery
- **Self-service**: Customer-facing portals and chatbots for common requests

## Customer Service Architecture

### Agent Desktop (Interaction Portal)
The agent desktop is the primary UI for service agents:

#### Desktop Components
- **Customer 360 view**: Consolidated view of the customer — demographics, accounts, products, interaction history, open cases, sentiment
- **Interaction panel**: Active chat/call details, customer context, channel information
- **Case management**: Create, view, and manage service cases
- **Knowledge panel**: AI-suggested knowledge articles relevant to the current issue
- **Next-Best-Action widget**: NBA-powered recommendations (offers, service suggestions, retention actions)
- **Pulse/collaboration**: Internal messaging and collaboration tools
- **Wrap-up**: Post-interaction summary and disposition

#### Agent Desktop Customization
- Configure which widgets appear and their layout
- Add custom widgets for organization-specific data
- Configure role-based views (Tier 1 vs Tier 2 vs supervisor)
- Integrate external tools and systems via embedded iframes or API calls

### Channel Management

#### Supported Channels
- **Voice (Phone)**: CTI integration (Genesys, Avaya, Amazon Connect, Cisco). Screen pop with customer context. Call controls (transfer, hold, conference).
- **Chat**: Web chat, mobile chat. Concurrent chat handling (agent handles multiple chats). Chat transfer and supervisor monitoring.
- **Email**: Inbound email parsing and case creation. Email threading. Template-based responses with merge fields.
- **Messaging**: Apple Business Chat, WhatsApp, Facebook Messenger, SMS. Asynchronous conversations.
- **Social**: Twitter, Facebook monitoring and response. Sentiment detection.
- **Web self-service**: Customer portal for case creation, tracking, and resolution.
- **IVR**: Interactive Voice Response integration for automated phone resolution.
- **Chatbot**: AI-powered conversational bot for common requests.

#### Channel Integration Architecture
- Channels connect to Pega via the Interaction Server or DX API
- Each channel creates an Interaction object that wraps the service case
- Channel-specific data (caller ID, chat transcript, email headers) is captured on the interaction
- Agents see a unified view regardless of the originating channel

### Service Case Types

#### Out-of-the-Box Case Types
- **Inquiry**: Customer has a question (billing, product, account)
- **Problem**: Something is wrong (service outage, defective product, incorrect charge)
- **Request**: Customer wants something done (address change, plan upgrade, cancellation)
- **Complaint**: Customer is dissatisfied and wants resolution
- **Dispute**: Customer disputes a charge or decision

#### Case Type Configuration
Each service case type has:
- **Stages**: Define the workflow (Investigation → Resolution → Confirmation)
- **SLAs**: Response time and resolution time targets per priority
- **Routing**: Skills-based routing to appropriate agents
- **Automations**: Auto-classification, auto-assignment, auto-resolution for simple cases
- **Knowledge**: Linked knowledge articles for common issues

## AI in Customer Service

### Next-Best-Action for Service
NBA in the service context recommends:
- **Service actions**: "Offer callback", "Escalate to specialist", "Send knowledge article"
- **Retention actions**: "Offer discount", "Waive fee", "Upgrade plan" (when customer shows churn signals)
- **Sales actions**: "Cross-sell product", "Mention promotion" (when appropriate during service interaction)
- **Self-service suggestions**: "Direct to FAQ", "Suggest mobile app feature"

### Pega Knowledge Management
- **Knowledge base**: Centralized repository of articles, FAQs, procedures
- **AI-suggested articles**: Based on case details, customer context, and interaction keywords, Pega suggests relevant articles to the agent
- **Customer-facing knowledge**: Articles published to self-service portal for customer access
- **Knowledge authoring**: Subject matter experts create and maintain articles with rich text, images, and video
- **Feedback loop**: Agents rate article helpfulness, driving continuous improvement

### Email Bot / Triage
- Pega reads incoming customer emails
- NLP extracts intent, sentiment, entities (account numbers, product names)
- Auto-creates appropriate case type based on detected intent
- Routes to the right team/agent based on content analysis
- For simple requests, auto-generates and sends a response

### Sentiment Analysis
- Real-time sentiment detection during live interactions (chat, messaging)
- Aggregated sentiment scoring based on recent interactions
- Sentiment-triggered actions: escalate if sentiment drops below threshold, offer retention if negative trend detected
- Historical sentiment trends visible in customer 360 view

### Agent AI Copilot
- **Response suggestions**: AI suggests response text for chat/email based on context
- **Wrap-up generation**: Auto-generates interaction summary and case notes
- **Next step guidance**: Suggests the next step in the workflow based on what the customer said
- **Translation**: Real-time translation for multilingual support

## Skills-Based Routing

### How It Works
1. **Define skills**: Language (English, Spanish), product area (Credit Card, Mortgage), issue type (Billing, Technical), certification level
2. **Assign skills to agents**: Each agent has a skill profile with proficiency levels
3. **Map case requirements to skills**: Based on case data, determine required skills
4. **Route to best-fit agent**: Match case requirements to available agents with the right skills
5. **Fallback**: If no exact match, broaden the search (lower proficiency threshold, expand skill criteria)

### Routing Strategies
- **Best-fit**: Route to the agent whose skills best match the case requirements
- **Least-occupied**: Route to the agent with the fewest active interactions
- **Round-robin**: Distribute evenly across qualified agents
- **Priority-based**: High-priority cases get routed to senior agents
- **Blended**: Combine skill match, availability, and occupancy for optimal assignment

### Routing Troubleshooting
- **Long queue times**: Not enough agents with required skills. Broaden skill requirements or train more agents.
- **Misrouted cases**: Skill mapping is incorrect. Review the case-to-skill mapping rules.
- **Uneven distribution**: Some agents overloaded while others idle. Check the routing strategy and agent availability status.

## Service Level Management

### SLA Configuration for Service
- **First response time**: Time to first meaningful response (e.g., 1 hour for P1, 4 hours for P2)
- **Resolution time**: Time to resolve the case (e.g., 4 hours for P1, 24 hours for P2, 72 hours for P3)
- **Escalation matrix**: Define who gets notified at each SLA milestone
- **Business hours**: Configure SLA calculation based on business hours, not clock hours
- **Priority matrix**: Derive case priority from impact × urgency

### Service Metrics & KPIs
- **First Contact Resolution (FCR)**: Percentage of cases resolved in the first interaction
- **Average Handle Time (AHT)**: Average duration of an interaction (talk time + after-call work)
- **Customer Satisfaction (CSAT)**: Post-interaction survey scores
- **Net Promoter Score (NPS)**: Likelihood of customer recommending the company
- **Backlog**: Number of open, unresolved cases
- **Abandonment rate**: Percentage of customers who hang up or leave chat before being served
- **Agent utilization**: Percentage of agent time spent on active interactions

## Self-Service

### Customer Self-Service Portal
- **Case creation**: Customer creates service requests without agent assistance
- **Case tracking**: View status of open cases and expected resolution time
- **Knowledge search**: Search and browse knowledge articles
- **Account management**: Update contact information, preferences, communication settings
- **Guided processes**: Step-by-step wizards for common tasks (file a claim, request a refund)

### Chatbot Integration
- **Intent detection**: NLP-based understanding of customer intent
- **Guided resolution**: Bot walks customer through troubleshooting steps
- **Data collection**: Bot collects case information before handing to agent (warm transfer)
- **Seamless escalation**: Transfer to live agent with full conversation context
- **Multi-language**: Bot supports multiple languages

## Customer Service Configuration

### Implementing a New Service Process
1. **Define case types**: Identify the types of service requests (inquiry, problem, request)
2. **Design workflows**: Map stages, steps, assignments, and SLAs for each case type
3. **Configure routing**: Set up skills-based routing with skill definitions and agent profiles
4. **Build knowledge**: Create knowledge articles for common issues
5. **Set up channels**: Configure channel connectors (CTI, chat, email)
6. **Configure NBA**: Set up service and retention actions for agent recommendations
7. **Build reports**: Create dashboards for service metrics and SLA compliance
8. **Test end-to-end**: Test each channel from customer initiation through case resolution

## Troubleshooting Customer Service

**Agent desktop not loading customer data**
- Check the data page sources for customer 360 view. Verify integration with CRM/core system.
- Check access control — does the agent's access group have permission to view customer data?
- Check for JavaScript errors in browser console (Constellation rendering issue).

**Chat not connecting**
- Verify the chat service is running and accessible.
- Check the Interaction Server configuration.
- Verify the web chat widget is properly embedded on the customer-facing page.
- Check firewall/proxy rules for WebSocket connections.

**Emails not creating cases**
- Check the email listener configuration — is it connected to the correct mailbox?
- Verify the email parsing rules are extracting intent correctly.
- Check the case creation data transform — are required fields being populated?
- Look for errors in the Pega log related to the email listener agent.

**SLA not triggering escalation**
- Verify the SLA agent is running (Admin Studio → Agents)
- Check SLA configuration — are the time intervals correct?
- Verify business calendar is configured if using business hours.
- Check that the escalation action is properly configured (email, reassignment, etc.).

## Interview & Certification Q&A

**Q: What is the difference between an Interaction and a Case in Pega Customer Service?**
A: An Interaction represents a single contact between a customer and the service center (a phone call, a chat session, an email exchange). A Case represents the underlying service request or issue that may span multiple interactions. One case can have many interactions (customer calls multiple times about the same issue), and one interaction can create or update multiple cases.

**Q: How does skills-based routing work?**
A: Cases are tagged with required skills based on their data (product, issue type, language). Agents have skill profiles with proficiency levels. The routing engine matches case requirements to available agents, selecting the best-fit agent. If no exact match exists, the system broadens criteria or uses fallback routing. This ensures customers reach the most qualified agent.

**Q: How does Pega Customer Service handle omnichannel interactions?**
A: All channels (voice, chat, email, messaging, social) connect through a unified integration layer. Each channel creates an Interaction object that wraps around the service case. The agent desktop provides a single view regardless of channel origin. Customer context and history are available regardless of which channel the customer used previously. Agents can handle multiple concurrent interactions across different channels.

**Q: What is First Contact Resolution and how does Pega help improve it?**
A: FCR is the percentage of cases resolved in the first interaction without requiring a callback or follow-up. Pega improves FCR through: AI-suggested knowledge articles (agent finds the answer faster), guided workflows (agent follows proven resolution paths), NBA recommendations (proactive resolution before the customer needs to call back), and agent copilot suggestions (AI-powered response drafting).
